gaps_ts_to_py.description: Crawlee TS has no first-party AWS Lambda deployment docs
gaps_ts_to_py.context_for_project: Epic 7 will run on Hetzner / Cloud Run, not Lambda; gap is non-blocking
gaps_ts_to_py.mitigation: Use community Lambda recipes if needed; or keep TS workers on long-running VPS

gaps_ts_to_py.description: Crawlee TS requires IPC or DB view to talk to rictom/cnpj-sqlite (Python lib)
gaps_ts_to_py.context_for_project: Real for the CNPJ subsystem only
gaps_ts_to_py.mitigation: Keep CNPJ ETL in its own Python container, expose via Supabase materialized views (Wave 2 decision)

gaps_ts_to_py.description: Data-science adjacency (pandas, polars, scikit-learn) requires either Python service or JS equivalents (Danfo.js, etc.)
gaps_ts_to_py.context_for_project: Wave A is heuristic determinístico, ML é Wave B — pode rodar em Python isolado
gaps_ts_to_py.mitigation: Same container split as CNPJ; ML lives in Python

gaps_py_to_ts.description: Crawlee Python has no StagehandCrawler (AI natural-language CSS selectors)
gaps_py_to_ts.context_for_project: Useful for sites that change CSS frequently (ImovelWeb, MercadoLivre)
gaps_py_to_ts.mitigation: Wait for Python port (Apify roadmap mentions cross-pollination) or build crawler-specific selectors

gaps_py_to_ts.description: AdaptivePlaywrightCrawler less mature in Python (v0.5 era, still feature-incomplete vs JS reference)
gaps_py_to_ts.context_for_project: Hybrid pattern is the primary cost lever for MercadoLivre/ImovelWeb (avoid browser when HTTP works)
gaps_py_to_ts.mitigation: Use Python only for sites where HTTP path is sufficient; reach for JS adaptive for harder targets

gaps_py_to_ts.description: Python smaller community + fewer first-party templates + fewer SO answers
gaps_py_to_ts.context_for_project: Time-to-resolve issues impacts Wave A timeline
gaps_py_to_ts.mitigation: Lean on Apify Discord; budget more time for novel issues
gaps_py_to_ts.source: GitHub API community metrics + Apify Discord

gaps_py_to_ts.description: Cannot share TypeScript domain types with Next.js (Real-State-Moema main app)
gaps_py_to_ts.context_for_project: Major friction for a project where the rest of the codebase is TS-first
gaps_py_to_ts.mitigation: Generate Python pydantic models from a shared schema (e.g., openapi-typescript + datamodel-code-generator) — extra tooling

gaps_py_to_ts.description: Two-runtime deploy = two CI lanes, two base images, two dependency lifecycles
gaps_py_to_ts.context_for_project: Operational overhead small team (founder + Luciana) cannot absorb
gaps_py_to_ts.mitigation: Isolate Python only where it pays for itself (CNPJ ETL)
gaps_py_to_ts.source: implicit from project Dockerfile + Vercel deploy

gaps_py_to_ts.description: GIL caveat for CPU-bound parsing in single-process; multi-process is doable but Operatable cost is higher
gaps_py_to_ts.context_for_project: Volume ~10-50k pgs/mês cabe em single-process JS
gaps_py_to_ts.mitigation: Use Python multiprocess pool only for ETL CNPJ batch

summary.interpretation: Both gaps with HIGH severity against Python are STACK-LEVEL (type sharing, runtime count). Gaps against TS are ALL contained in the CNPJ subsystem which already lives in Python.
